canonical_text:
L2_AESOP_001-S01: The Fox and the Grapes
L2_AESOP_001-S02: A FAMISHED FOX saw some clusters of ripe black grapes hanging from a trellised vine. She resorted to all her tricks to get at them, but wearied herself in vain, for she could not reach them. At last she turned away, hiding her disappointment and saying: “The Grapes are sour, and not ripe as I thought.”
edition: Project Gutenberg eBook #21, Three Hundred Aesop’s Fables
rights_note: Execution-preparation packet uses public-domain Project Gutenberg source text. Public release or redistribution outside this repository review still requires source-specific rights audit.
